expectations.evidence: The email begins with 'Hi Bob,' on line 3 of email.md, which is a clear greeting addressing the recipient by name.
The email ends with 'Best regards' on line 9 of email.md, which is a professional sign-off.

expectations.text: The output is properly formatted with paragraphs
expectations.evidence: The email is structured with distinct paragraphs separated by blank lines: a subject line (line 1), greeting (line 3), body paragraph (line 5), call-to-action paragraph (line 7), and closing (line 9).

claims.claim: The email replaces informal tone with professional language
claims.evidence: The casual 'Hey bob' becomes 'Hi Bob,' and 'they look off to me' becomes 'a few figures appear to be inconsistent with what I would expect'. The tone is consistently professional throughout.

claims.claim: The email includes a call to action (scheduling a meeting)
claims.evidence: Line 7: 'Please let me know your availability, and I will be happy to set up a meeting.'

claims.claim: The email includes a clear subject line
claims.evidence: Line 1: 'Subject: Follow-Up on Q4 Financial Figures'

claims.claim: The sign-off is missing the sender's name
claims.evidence: The email ends with 'Best regards' but does not include a sender name or signature block, which is incomplete for a professional email.

suggestions.reason: This is trivially satisfied — even a malformed output containing the word 'Hello' anywhere would pass. Consider checking that the greeting addresses the recipient by name ('Bob') and appears at the start of the email body.
Similarly weak — any text containing 'regards' or 'sincerely' anywhere would pass. Consider verifying the sign-off appears at the end and includes a sender name.

No assertion checks that the original message content (Q4 numbers, concern about discrepancies) is preserved in the output. An email about a completely different topic would pass all three assertions.
No assertion checks that the output is actually an email format (has Subject line, greeting, body, closing) rather than just any text with paragraphs and a greeting somewhere in it.

eval_feedback.overall: All three assertions are surface-level presence checks that could be satisfied by almost any professional-looking text, regardless of whether it correctly transforms the input. The evals need content-preservation assertions and structural format checks to be discriminating.
